title: The Ridley Scott Hall of Fame. Plus: Errol Morris!
format: Sean, Amanda, and Chris Ryan (on his birthday) build a 10-film Ridley Scott Hall of Fame, then Sean talks to Errol Morris about The Pigeon Tunnel.

films.title: Napoleon
films.note: Ruled ineligible for the Hall of Fame since it hasn't opened yet, but they just saw it at a DGA screening and can't stop talking about it.

segments.heading: The Ridley Scott Hall of Fame
segments.note: reds are out, yellows are maybes, greens are in, worked chronologically through the filmography

films.title: The Duellists
films.note: Red. A cool artifact of his ad-director years, but a filmography this stacked doesn't have room for it.

films.title: Alien
films.note: Green. The greenest green: he saw Star Wars, panicked, and reinvented science-fiction filmmaking to answer it.

films.title: Blade Runner
films.note: Green. Cold, metallic, endlessly re-cut since release, and it anticipated how obsessively we'd pick movies apart online.

films.title: Legend
films.note: Red. Tom Cruise as a mythological hero in the land of nymphs. Just not good.

films.title: Someone to Watch Over Me
films.note: Red. His American-noir cop thriller. Looks good, isn't terribly strong.

films.title: Black Rain
films.note: Red. Michael Douglas versus the Yakuza. A pretty good cable movie from the mid-'80s lull when people had started writing him off.

films.title: Thelma & Louise
films.note: Green. Auto-green. His John Ford movie, but with two women driving across Monument Valley toward freedom.

films.title: 1492: Conquest of Paradise
films.note: Red. Gerard Depardieu as Christopher Columbus. Rewatched for House of Gucci prep and it's terrible.

films.title: White Squall
films.note: Yellow. An underrated coming-of-age movie with Jeff Bridges running a reform school on a yacht. Doesn't survive to green.

films.title: G.I. Jane
films.note: Red. Bombed at the time, but a dry run for the leaner, more character-focused mode he'd return to.

films.title: Gladiator
films.note: Green. Auto-green. The Best Picture winner that turned Russell Crowe into a movie star and re-anointed Scott himself.

films.title: Hannibal
films.note: Red. Grotesque, entertaining, not a green.

films.title: Black Hawk Down
films.note: Green. A war film with a cast that doubles as a graduating class of movie stars for the next two decades.

films.title: Matchstick Men
films.note: Yellow. A personal favorite for Sean and one of his better scripts, but doesn't crack the final ten.

films.title: Kingdom of Heaven
films.note: Green, via the four-hour director's cut. The theatrical release was a shrug; the director's cut is a Lawrence of Arabia-scale epic that earns one of the last two slots.

films.title: A Good Year
films.note: Red. The one he made because he'd bought a vineyard in Provence and fell in love with it.

films.title: American Gangster
films.note: Green, promoted from yellow. Suffered at release from Godfather-sized expectations; the Denzel-Crowe interrogation scene alone earns it the other last slot.

films.title: Body of Lies
films.note: Red. A gripping first hour, then a romance subplot sinks it.

films.title: Robin Hood
films.note: Red. A movie no one needed. Started life as a revisionist take called Nottingham before turning into basically Gladiator in England.

films.title: Prometheus
films.note: Green. "Green as fuck." Upsetting, gorgeous, and made partly to keep the Alien franchise out of Neill Blomkamp's hands.

films.title: The Counselor
films.note: Green. A tribute to his late brother Tony and, per Sean and Chris, one of the great ones.

films.title: Exodus: Gods and Kings
films.note: Red. His Cecil B. DeMille movie, made and released the same week of December as Prometheus two years earlier. A lot of bad ideas meeting in a desert.

films.title: The Martian
films.note: Green. An absolute green, and the rare Scott movie where he visibly lets everyone in the cast cook.

films.title: All the Money in the World
films.note: Red. The Kevin Spacey reshoot movie. Christopher Plummer got an Oscar nomination out of the replacement performance anyway.

films.title: Alien: Covenant
films.note: Red. Rip-roaring first two acts, a confusing back half. Still, by their math, the fourth-best Alien movie.

films.title: The Last Duel
films.note: Yellow. Grim and punishing to sit through, but a great script from Damon and Affleck (with Nicole Holofcener).

films.title: House of Gucci
films.note: Red on the show, green in Sean's heart. The campy romp they don't regret hyping.

films.title: The Pigeon Tunnel
films.note: His documentary about John le Carre, built around an interview where le Carre spends as much time interrogating Morris as the other way around.

referenced.group: Errol Morris's other films, mentioned in passing

films: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword
The Spy Who Came in from the Cold
